title: Policy Futures Programme 2026–2027 in the UK: Earn a £30,000 Salary While Launching Your Career in Public Policy Research
summary: Are you passionate about shaping public policy, influencing decision-making, and tackling some of the UK s biggest social challenges? The Policy Futures Programme 2026–2027 offers an exceptional opportunity for aspiring policy professionals to gain hands-on experience, structured training, and a salaried position within some of the United Kingdom s leading think tanks. Delivered by The Ladder Group in partnership with IPPR (Institute for Public Policy Research) , Demos , and the Social Market Foundation (SMF) , the programme has been created to make careers in policy research more accessible—particularly for talented individuals from lower socioeconomic backgrounds who have traditionally been underrepresented in the policy sector. Successful participants will receive a £30,000 annual salary , extensive professional training, practical work experience, networking opportunities, and exposure to influential organizations involved in policy development across the UK. If you are looking to begin a meaningful career in public policy, research, government, or social impact, this programme provides an outstanding entry point. About the Policy Futures Programme The Policy Futures Programme was established with a clear mission: to ensure that the people shaping public policy in Britain better reflect the diversity of modern society. Public policy influences nearly every aspect of life—from education and healthcare to housing, employment, climate action, and economic development. Despite its importance, careers in policy research have historically been difficult to access for individuals from disadvantaged socioeconomic backgrounds. This programme seeks to remove those barriers by providing participants with paid employment, structured learning, mentoring, and professional development within respected UK think tanks. Participants will spend one year working with one of the programme s partner organizations: Institute for Public Policy Research (IPPR) Demos Social Market Foundation (SMF) Throughout the programme, participants will contribute to real-world policy projects while developing the practical skills required for long-term careers in research and policymaking. What You Will Do Participants will work as part of professional policy research teams and contribute to projects addressing major national and regional issues. Your responsibilities may include the following: 1. Policy Research You will help gather and evaluate evidence that informs policy recommendations by: Reviewing academic publications. Reading expert reports. Conducting background research. Supporting survey design. Assisting with interviews and focus groups. Collecting information from multiple reliable sources. 2. Data Analysis Participants will work with both qualitative and quantitative data to: Identify important trends. Analyse public policy issues. Interpret research findings. Support evidence-based decision-making. 3. Developing Policy Solutions Working alongside experienced researchers and fellow participants, you will: Generate practical policy ideas. Develop solutions to social and economic challenges. Use research findings to support recommendations. Contribute to projects influencing public debate. 4. Communications and Report Writing Strong communication is central to policy work. During the programme you will learn to: Write policy reports. Present research findings. Communicate complex ideas clearly. Prepare briefing documents. Produce content suitable for policymakers, stakeholders, and the general public. 5. Event Delivery Participants will also support the organization of: Policy roundtables. Public discussions. Networking events. Stakeholder meetings. Presentations showcasing research findings. Professional Development and Training One of the biggest strengths of the Policy Futures Programme is its structured learning experience. Throughout the year, participants receive training in: Research methods. Policy development. Evidence-based analysis. Stakeholder engagement. Professional communication. Report writing. Public presentation skills. These skills are highly transferable and valuable across government, charities, research institutions, consultancy firms, and the wider public and private sectors. External Placement Opportunities Participants will also have the opportunity to undertake an external placement with another think tank. This allows Fellows to: Experience different policy environments. Understand different research approaches. Expand professional networks. Learn how policy is developed across multiple organizations. Exposure to the Wider Policy Sector In addition to working within their host think tank, participants will gain valuable insight into organizations involved in public policy, including: Charities. Local authorities. Regional authorities. National government institutions. Advocacy organizations. This broad exposure provides participants with a comprehensive understanding of how public policy is researched, designed, promoted, and implemented. Career Outcomes By the end of the programme, participants will have: Contributed to meaningful policy research. Developed highly sought-after research skills. Built a professional network. Strengthened communication abilities. Gained practical experience in public policy. Improved stakeholder engagement skills. Enhanced career prospects across public, private, and third-sector organizations. Graduates of the programme will be well positioned for careers in: Public policy. Government. Research. Think tanks. Non-governmental organizations. Charities. Consultancy. Public affairs. Social impact organizations. Salary and Benefits Successful participants will receive: Annual salary of £30,000 Employee benefits provided by their host organization Structured professional training Career development opportunities Mentorship and networking opportunities Who Should Apply? The programme is specifically designed for people who may not traditionally have had access to careers in policy research. No previous experience in policy or politics is required. Applicants should demonstrate qualities such as: Research Skills You should be able to: Gather information. Analyse evidence. Interpret findings. Reach logical conclusions. This experience may come from university, work, volunteering, or personal projects. Communication Skills Applicants should be able to: Communicate clearly in writing. Speak confidently with different audiences. Work effectively with people from diverse backgrounds and political perspectives. Diligence Successful candidates are expected to be: Well organised. Detail-oriented. Good at managing priorities. Curiosity The programme values individuals who are: Eager to learn. Interested in current policy debates. Open to constructive feedback. Desire to Make a Difference Applicants should be motivated to help solve important challenges facing UK society. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must satisfy at least one of the following criteria: Come from a low-income or lower socioeconomic background. Be the first person in their family to attend university. Have been eligible for free school meals during their education. The programme also particularly welcomes applications from candidates from racial or ethnic minority backgrounds. Right to Work Requirement Applicants must have the unrestricted legal right to work in the United Kingdom for the full programme period. The programme runs from: August 2026 to September 2027 Visa sponsorship is generally not available . Educational Background No specific degree or academic discipline is required. Eligible applicants include: Final-year university students. Recent graduates. Apprenticeship graduates. Individuals without university or apprenticeship qualifications who can demonstrate relevant skills through work, volunteering, or other experiences. Applicants from non-traditional educational pathways are strongly encouraged to apply. Work Experience Requirements The programme is intended for individuals entering the policy sector. You are not eligible if you have: More than three months of formal paid work experience in policy or politics. Previously worked for similar organizations in the policy sector. Instead, experienced applicants are encouraged to apply directly for trainee researcher positions within partner organizations. Application Process Applicants must complete the online application and submit: An up-to-date CV. Three written responses of no more than 200 words each . Applicants must answer the following questions: Why are you interested in the Policy Futures Programme? What aspect of your education, work experience, or extracurricular activities has prepared you for the programme? What current issue facing the UK would you like a think tank to focus on? AI Usage Policy Applicants may use Artificial Intelligence tools for brainstorming, organizing ideas, or conducting research. However, written responses should be entirely your own work. Applications containing AI-generated written answers are unlikely to progress because the programme aims to assess each applicant s genuine interest, communication skills, and authentic voice. Application Deadline Applications close on 26 June at 12:30 PM. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, meaning interviews take place as applications are received. Candidates are therefore encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible. Programme Summary Item Details Programme Policy Futures Programme 2026–2027 Host The Ladder Group (with IPPR, Demos and SMF) Location United Kingdom Salary £30,000 per year Programme Duration August 2026 – September 2027 Experience Required None Visa Sponsorship Generally unavailable Application Requirement CV + three 200-word responses Deadline 26 June, 12:30 PM Apply Apply Here Final Thoughts The Policy Futures Programme is an outstanding opportunity for aspiring policy professionals who want to build a meaningful career while helping shape the future of the United Kingdom. Through paid employment, structured training, practical experience, and collaboration with leading think tanks, participants will gain the knowledge, skills, and confidence needed to thrive in policy research and public affairs. If you meet the eligibility requirements and are passionate about making a difference, submit your application as early as possible before the 26 June at 12:30 PM deadline to maximize your chances of being considered. For more information, visit the official call for applications Discover more global job opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post Policy Futures Programme 2026–2027 in the UK: Earn a £30,000 Salary While Launching Your Career in Public Policy Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Smithsonian Hirshhorn Museum Internship 2026 in Washington, DC | Paid Museum & Archives Opportunity for International Students
summary: The Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum and Sculpture Garden has officially opened applications for its highly sought-after Exhibition History Cataloging Internship 2026 in Washington, DC. This exciting paid internship offers undergraduate students, graduate students, and recent graduates the opportunity to gain hands-on museum experience at one of the world’s leading modern and contemporary art museums. Hosted by the Documentation and Archives Department within the Collection Care and Management division, the internship provides practical exposure to archival research, museum cataloging systems, collection management, and historical exhibition documentation. Participants will work directly with museum records and databases while developing professional research and archival skills in a globally respected institution. Applications are open until July 24, 2026. About the Smithsonian Hirshhorn Museum Internship 2026 The Hirshhorn Museum and Sculpture Garden is part of the Smithsonian Institution and is internationally recognized for its significant collection of modern and contemporary art. Through this internship, selected candidates will contribute to the preservation and organization of historical exhibition records while learning museum documentation procedures used by professional archivists and collection managers. The internship specifically focuses on exhibition history cataloging and database management using TMS (The Museum System), a professional museum collections database widely used across museums and cultural institutions. This internship is ideal for students and emerging professionals interested in: Museum studies Art history Archives and records management Collection documentation Research and cataloging Cultural heritage preservation Internship Responsibilities Selected interns will support the Documentation and Archives Department with several important museum tasks, including: 1. Exhibition Record Cataloging Interns will add historic exhibition records and exhibition checklists into the museum’s cataloging database system. 2. Database Documentation Participants will learn how museums organize and maintain historical records using professional cataloging software and archival standards. 3. Research and Verification Interns will review exhibition paper records, publications, and archival materials to ensure historical accuracy before entering data into the museum database. 4. Checklist Creation and Cross-Referencing The internship includes training in: Checklist development Cross-reference techniques Metadata organization Historical documentation methods 5. Archival and Museum Practice Exposure Interns will gain first-hand understanding of: Museum archival procedures Collection care operations Documentation standards Professional museum workflows Internship Benefits The Smithsonian Hirshhorn Internship offers several valuable academic and professional benefits for participants. Financial Support Selected interns will receive a stipend of $3,200. Professional Experience Participants will gain hands-on experience working in a Smithsonian museum environment and build practical skills in: Museum cataloging Archival research Documentation systems Art history research Database management Career Development This internship can strengthen future applications for: Museum careers Graduate school programs Art history programs Archival studies Library and information science careers Cultural heritage institutions Networking Opportunities Interns will work closely with museum professionals and gain insight into the operations of one of the world’s most respected museum institutions. Internship Duration and Schedule Internship Start Date: September 28, 2026 Internship End Date: November 20, 2026 Duration: 8 weeks Weekly Commitment: 16 hours per week Work Format: On-site Location: Washington, DC Work schedules and internship hours will be arranged mutually between the selected intern and the internship supervisor before the program begins. Who Can Apply? The internship is open to: Currently enrolled undergraduate students Graduate students Doctoral students Recent graduates who completed their degree within one year prior to the internship start date Students graduating during the internship period International applicants are encouraged to apply, and there is no citizenship requirement. Selection Criteria Applicants will be evaluated based on: Demonstrated academic interest in museums, archives, or collection management Career goals aligned with museum and archival work Interest in art history research Attention to detail and research capability Required Application Materials Applicants must prepare and submit the following documents through the Smithsonian Online Academic Appointment System (SOLAA): Online application form Two letters of reference Academic transcripts (unofficial transcripts accepted) Personal essay addressing: Why you are interested in this internship How the opportunity will advance your academic or professional goals Important Dates Application Deadline: July 24, 2026 Decision Notifications: August 3–7, 2026 Internship Start Date: September 28, 2026 Internship End Date: November 20, 2026 Why This Internship Matters The Smithsonian Hirshhorn Internship is more than a short-term placement. It is an opportunity to gain practical museum experience within one of the world’s most respected cultural institutions. For students interested in museum careers, archives, cultural preservation, or art history, this internship provides direct exposure to professional museum documentation practices while helping participants develop valuable research, cataloging, and archival skills. Because the program welcomes international applicants and offers a stipend, it is also an excellent opportunity for students worldwide seeking international museum experience in the United States. How to Apply for the Smithsonian Hirshhorn Internship 2026 To apply, candidates must complete their application through SOLAA. Application Steps Create an account or log in to SOLAA Click the “Start Application” button Search for the program Select: “Hirshhorn Exhibition History Cataloging Internship” Click “Apply Now” Upload all required documents and submit the application before the deadline Learn More Apply Click HERE to learn more and apply for the Smithsonian Hirshhorn Museum Internship 2026 in Washington, DC. Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE . The post Smithsonian Hirshhorn Museum Internship 2026 in Washington, DC | Paid Museum Archives Opportunity for International Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: The British Library Joanna and Graham Barker Fellowship Scheme 2026–2027 | Fully Funded Postdoctoral Fellowship Opportunities in the UK
summary: Applications Open for Two Prestigious British Library Research Fellowships The British Library has officially launched applications for the highly anticipated Joanna and Graham Barker Fellowship Scheme 2026–2027, offering two funded fellowship opportunities for postdoctoral early-career researchers who are passionate about cultural heritage, archives, artificial intelligence, digital discovery, and historical collections. With applications closing on 15 July 2026 , this fellowship programme provides researchers with a unique opportunity to work directly with one of the world’s most important research libraries while collaborating with expert curators, archivists, and specialist research teams. The 12-month fellowship programme is designed to bridge the gap between doctoral study and long-term professional research careers by equipping fellows with advanced practical, interdisciplinary, and future-focused skills within the cultural and heritage sector. The fellowship will officially begin from 1 September 2026 , with part-time arrangements potentially extending the fellowship duration. About the Joanna and Graham Barker Fellowship Scheme The Joanna and Graham Barker Fellowship Scheme was established through a generous donation from Joanna and Graham Barker to support postdoctoral researchers at a critical stage in their academic and professional journey. The initiative focuses on helping early-career researchers gain: Practical institutional experience Research leadership skills Project management exposure Interdisciplinary collaboration opportunities Cultural heritage sector expertise Professional mentoring and development Access to world-class archival collections The British Library aims to create a structured pathway for emerging researchers to thrive within the rapidly evolving knowledge economy and research landscape. This fellowship particularly addresses the significant funding gap faced by postdoctoral researchers across academia and the cultural sector. Fellowship Research Themes for 2026 Applicants are invited to co-design a research project with the British Library based on one of the following two specialist themes. 1. Colonial Legal Deposit and the British Library Newspaper Collection This fellowship is based within the: News Moving Image Department Sound Vision Teams Research Focus The fellowship explores the historical and contemporary implications of colonial legal deposit systems and their relationship with newspaper collections preserved by the British Library. Potential areas of exploration may include: Colonial-era newspaper acquisition systems Historical legal deposit practices Representation and preservation of colonial narratives Media archives and empire studies Accessibility of historical newspaper collections Decolonisation of archival practices Historical metadata and discoverability Newspaper preservation and interpretation Researchers interested in: Media history Colonial studies Digital archives Historical collections Cultural memory Information preservation may find this theme especially relevant. 2. Local Large Language Models for Personal Digital Archives This fellowship is based within: Contemporary British and Irish Archives and Manuscripts Research Focus The second fellowship investigates how Local Large Language Models (LLMs) can improve the discovery, accessibility, interpretation, and management of personal digital archives. The project aligns with the growing importance of artificial intelligence in archives and cultural heritage institutions. Possible research areas include: Ethical AI implementation in archives Local LLM development AI-powered archival discovery tools Digital preservation Metadata enhancement Privacy-aware AI systems Personal digital archive accessibility Human-centered archival technologies Machine learning for cultural heritage collections This opportunity is ideal for researchers working in: Artificial Intelligence Digital Humanities Archival Science Computational Humanities Library and Information Science Machine Learning Cultural Informatics Digital Preservation Fellowship Benefits and Opportunities Selected fellows will receive an extensive range of professional and research benefits throughout the programme. Key Fellowship Advantages 1. Fully Funded Research Opportunity Participants will undertake a funded 12-month fellowship embedded within the British Library. 2. Collaboration with Leading Experts Fellows will work closely with: Curators Archivists Research specialists Digital humanities professionals Cultural heritage experts 3. Access to World-Class Collections Researchers will gain privileged access to: British Library archival collections Newspaper repositories Manuscripts Digital archives Cultural heritage datasets 4. Professional Development The fellowship includes: Research training Project management experience Leadership development Interdisciplinary collaboration Institutional research exposure 5. Career Advancement The scheme is specifically designed to strengthen the long-term career prospects of postdoctoral researchers across academia and the GLAM sector. Why This Fellowship Matters The British Library highlights that postdoctoral researchers often face limited funding opportunities after completing their PhDs. Research studies show that career precarity disproportionately affects: Women researchers Researchers of colour Researchers with disabilities First-generation academics Researchers with caring responsibilities This fellowship directly addresses those challenges by creating inclusive and sustainable research pathways. Main Objectives of the Fellowship Scheme The programme aims to: Build upon the success of collaborative doctoral programmes Support the transition toward independent research careers Expand opportunities in the cultural and heritage sector Strengthen interdisciplinary research practices Enhance discoverability and interpretation of collections Promote equality, diversity, and inclusion in research Generate impactful institutional knowledge Improve public engagement with cultural collections Fellowship Duration Duration: 12 months Start Date: 1 September 2026 Flexible part-time arrangements may extend the duration Eligibility Criteria To apply for the British Library Joanna and Graham Barker Fellowship Scheme, applicants must meet all of the following requirements: Mandatory Requirements Applicants must: Hold a doctorate in a relevant subject OR Possess equivalent professional experience and skills Applicants must also: Demonstrate commitment to working with the cultural and heritage sector Be based in the UK Be eligible to work in the UK for the full fellowship duration Self-identify as an early-career postdoctoral researcher according to AHRC guidance Important PhD Completion Requirement Applicants who have: Passed their viva with minor corrections before the deadline are eligible However: Applicants requiring major corrections that extend beyond the application deadline are not eligible. Equality, Diversity and Inclusion Commitment The British Library strongly promotes inclusive talent development and equitable research opportunities across: Galleries Libraries Archives Museums (GLAM sector) The fellowship prioritises accessibility, diversity, and broad participation in cultural heritage research. Application Deadline Deadline: 15 July 2026 Interested applicants should prepare their proposals early and carefully review the research themes before applying. Who Should Apply? This opportunity is highly suitable for: Postdoctoral researchers Digital humanities scholars Archivists AI and machine learning researchers Library and information science professionals Cultural heritage practitioners Humanities researchers Computational humanities specialists Media historians Archive technologists Final Thoughts The Joanna and Graham Barker Fellowship Scheme 2026–2027 represents an exceptional opportunity for early-career researchers seeking to combine academic excellence with real-world cultural heritage impact. By embedding fellows within the British Library’s expert teams and collections, the programme creates a rare environment where cutting-edge research, historical archives, artificial intelligence, and interdisciplinary collaboration intersect. For researchers aiming to build a meaningful career in academia, digital humanities, archival innovation, or cultural heritage institutions, this fellowship provides both the platform and professional support needed to advance their work on an international level. Learn More Apply Applicants must submit a research proposal aligned with one of the two fellowship themes. Click HERE to learn more and apply for the The British Library Joanna and Graham Barker Fellowship Scheme 2026–2027. Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE . The post The British Library Joanna and Graham Barker Fellowship Scheme 2026–2027 | Fully Funded Postdoctoral Fellowship Opportunities in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Women-Led Business Grant 2026: Apply for Up to $750,000 in Non-Dilutive Funding for Women Entrepreneurs Worldwide
summary: Women entrepreneurs around the world now have an outstanding opportunity to secure catalytic funding to scale their businesses through the Women-Led Business Grant 2026 . The program is currently accepting applications from women-led and women-owned businesses seeking financial support to expand their impact, strengthen operations, and accelerate sustainable growth. The grant provides non-dilutive funding ranging from $25,000 to $750,000 , allowing entrepreneurs to grow their ventures without giving up ownership or equity. In addition to funding, selected businesses will receive leadership coaching and access to a global peer network designed to support long-term business success. If you are a woman entrepreneur leading an innovative business with growth potential, this funding opportunity could help you expand into new markets, improve operational capacity, invest in technology, and create lasting economic impact. About the Women-Led Business Grant The Women-Led Business Grant is designed to support businesses that are founded, owned, or led by women. The initiative recognizes the important role women entrepreneurs play in driving innovation, creating employment opportunities, strengthening economies, and promoting inclusive growth across industries. Beyond financial support, the program offers valuable resources that help businesses become more competitive and sustainable. Successful applicants benefit from: Non-dilutive grant funding Leadership coaching Access to a global network of entrepreneurs Opportunities to accelerate business growth Support for businesses across all sectors The program is open to eligible businesses globally, making it an excellent opportunity for women entrepreneurs from both developed and emerging markets. Funding Amount Successful applicants may receive funding between: Minimum Grant: $25,000 Maximum Grant: $750,000 The amount awarded will depend on the project s scope, business needs, and overall impact potential. What Can the Grant Fund? The Women-Led Business Grant supports a wide variety of business activities that contribute to growth, innovation, and sustainability. Funding may be used for: 1. Working Capital Businesses can strengthen day-to-day operations by covering operational expenses and maintaining healthy cash flow. 2. Pilot Deployments Entrepreneurs can test new products, services, technologies, or business models before scaling them. 3. Technical Assistance and Capacity Building Grant funding can be invested in strengthening organizational systems, improving management practices, and building staff capacity. 4. Climate and ESG-Aligned Investments Businesses working on environmentally sustainable or socially responsible initiatives can use the funding to advance climate resilience and Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) goals. 5. Market Research and Feasibility Studies Applicants may conduct market assessments, customer research, and feasibility analyses before entering new markets or launching new products. 6. Equipment and Technology Procurement Businesses can purchase machinery, digital tools, software, equipment, and technology needed to improve productivity and competitiveness. 7. Monitoring, Evaluation, and Learning Funding may also support systems that measure business performance, project outcomes, and organizational impact for continuous improvement. Eligibility Requirements To qualify, businesses must meet at least one of the following requirements: Be at least 51% owned by women , or Have women serving in C-suite leadership positions . These eligibility requirements ensure that the funding directly supports women-led enterprises and promotes female leadership in business. Why You Should Apply The Women-Led Business Grant offers much more than financial assistance. Key benefits include: Access to grant funding without giving up company ownership. Leadership coaching from experienced professionals. Global networking opportunities with fellow entrepreneurs. Support for businesses operating in any industry. Funding for innovation, technology, sustainability, and business expansion. Opportunity to strengthen long-term organizational capacity. Increased visibility and credibility for supported businesses. Application Process Applying for the Women-Led Business Grant is designed to be straightforward. The application process includes: Complete the online application. Submit the required business information. Describe how the requested funding will be used. Submit your application for review. The application is estimated to take approximately 15 minutes to complete. Applicants can expect funding decisions within 4 to 6 weeks after submission. Application Fee There are no application fees required to apply for this grant. Program Standards The grant program operates according to World Bank Group standards , ensuring a structured and transparent funding process. Application Deadline Deadline: August 20, 2026 Interested applicants are encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid last-minute issues. Grant Summary Item Details Grant Name Women-Led Business Grant Funding $25,000–$750,000 Region Global Status Open Application Fee None Application Time Approximately 15 minutes Decision Timeline 4–6 weeks Deadline August 20, 2026 Apply Click here to apply Final Thoughts Access to growth capital remains one of the greatest challenges facing women entrepreneurs worldwide. The Women-Led Business Grant addresses this challenge by providing significant non-dilutive funding alongside leadership development and international networking opportunities. Whether your business is looking to expand operations, invest in technology, conduct research, improve sustainability, or strengthen organizational capacity, this grant offers a valuable opportunity to accelerate growth while maintaining ownership of your company. Eligible women-led businesses are encouraged to prepare their applications early and submit before the August 20, 2026 deadline. For more information, visit the official website Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post Women-Led Business Grant 2026: Apply for Up to $750,000 in Non-Dilutive Funding for Women Entrepreneurs Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fully Funded PhD Scholarship at UOW 2026: Research Gender-Based Violence in the Workplace
summary: The University of Wollongong (UOW) has announced an exciting PhD scholarship opportunity for researchers passionate about addressing one of the most critical social and workplace issues of our time — gender-based violence in the context of work. This prestigious research opportunity invites motivated scholars to contribute to groundbreaking academic work exploring how gender-based violence impacts workplaces, employment systems, professional environments, and worker wellbeing across different industries and communities. Applications and research proposals are due on June 29, 2026. About the PhD Scholarship This PhD scholarship at UOW is designed for emerging researchers who are committed to producing impactful, socially relevant, and policy-oriented research. The project focuses specifically on gender-based violence within workplace and employment contexts, an issue increasingly recognized globally as a major human rights, social justice, and labor concern. The scholarship presents an opportunity for candidates to work closely with academic experts while contributing to research that can influence workplace policies, organizational cultures, legal frameworks, and support systems for workers affected by violence and discrimination. Researchers selected for this program will have the opportunity to: Conduct high-level academic research Explore workplace-related gender inequalities and violence Develop evidence-based recommendations Contribute to policy discussions and reform Engage with interdisciplinary perspectives Build expertise in a globally relevant research area Research Focus Areas Applicants may explore a wide range of themes related to gender-based violence and work. Potential research areas may include: Workplace harassment and discrimination Gendered violence in professional settings Power dynamics and labor exploitation Organizational responses to workplace violence Domestic violence and its impact on employment Gender inequality in labor systems Psychological and emotional impacts on workers Legal protections and workplace policies Prevention and intervention frameworks Vulnerable workers and marginalized communities Intersectionality in workplace violence experiences Global and comparative workplace studies Candidates are encouraged to develop innovative and impactful proposals aligned with these broader themes. Why This Opportunity Matters Gender-based violence continues to affect millions of individuals worldwide, including within workplaces and employment systems. Research in this field has become increasingly important for governments, institutions, employers, and advocacy organizations seeking to create safer and more equitable professional environments. This PhD scholarship offers scholars the chance to contribute meaningful research that can: Influence workplace policies Support social justice initiatives Improve worker protections Inform government and institutional reforms Enhance organizational accountability Promote inclusive and safe work cultures The research outcomes from this program could have long-term academic, professional, and societal impact. Benefits of the Scholarship The University of Wollongong provides an excellent academic and research environment for doctoral researchers. Although applicants should review the official scholarship advertisement for exact funding details and eligibility requirements, benefits may include: Tuition fee support Research funding assistance Academic supervision and mentorship Access to university research facilities Networking and collaboration opportunities Professional development support Opportunities to publish and present research internationally Applicants are strongly encouraged to review the official scholarship advertisement carefully for precise funding information and conditions. Who Should Apply? This opportunity is ideal for: Graduates seeking to pursue doctoral research Researchers passionate about gender justice and workplace equity Individuals interested in social policy and labor issues Academics aiming to influence real-world workplace reform Professionals with backgrounds in sociology, law, psychology, gender studies, public policy, labor relations, or related fields Strong research, analytical, and proposal-writing skills will be highly valuable for applicants. Application Process Interested candidates are encouraged to prepare a strong and well-structured research proposal that clearly demonstrates: The relevance of the research topic Proposed research objectives Methodology and theoretical framework Potential contribution to knowledge and policy Alignment with the scholarship theme Applicants should carefully follow all submission instructions provided in the official scholarship advertisement. Important Deadline Application and proposal submission deadline: June 29, 2026 Applicants are advised to begin preparing their research proposals as early as possible to ensure a competitive submission. Final Thoughts The UOW PhD Scholarship on gender-based violence in the context of work represents an outstanding opportunity for aspiring researchers to engage in meaningful and impactful scholarship. As workplace safety, equity, and inclusion continue to gain global attention, research in this field has the potential to shape policies, improve worker wellbeing, and drive lasting institutional change. Candidates passionate about social justice, workplace reform, and gender equity are strongly encouraged to apply and contribute to this important area of research. For further information, applicants should review the official scholarship advertisement and contact the relevant coordinators with any questions regarding the opportunity. Learn More Apply Click HERE to learn more and apply for the Fully Funded PhD Scholarship at UOW 2026. Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE . title: Bow Seat True Blue Fellowship 2026: Global Fellowship for Young Ocean, Waterway, and Climate Change Leaders (Apply by August 30, 2026)
summary: Young people around the world who are passionate about protecting the ocean, waterways, and the climate now have an exciting opportunity to transform their ideas into meaningful action through the Bow Seat True Blue Fellowship 2026 . This prestigious international fellowship empowers young changemakers who use the creative arts as a powerful tool to address environmental challenges and inspire positive action within their communities. If you have an innovative project that combines creativity with environmental conservation, the True Blue Fellowship offers a platform to develop your leadership skills, collaborate with like-minded youth from across the globe, and receive mentorship while implementing your project. Applications are currently open, and interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline. About the Bow Seat True Blue Fellowship The True Blue Fellowship is one of Bow Seat Creative Action for Conservation s flagship youth leadership programs. It is designed to support young environmental leaders who are committed to safeguarding the ocean, waterways, and the global climate through creative and community-driven initiatives. The fellowship recognizes that art has the power to educate, inspire, and mobilize communities toward environmental action. Rather than focusing solely on scientific solutions, the program encourages applicants to combine creativity with leadership, advocacy, education, storytelling, and community engagement. Successful Fellows become part of the Future Blue Youth Council (FBYC), where they collaborate with other young environmental leaders from different countries while receiving support from Bow Seat staff and partners. Why You Should Apply The Bow Seat True Blue Fellowship is much more than a recognition program. It is an opportunity to join an international network of young environmental leaders working together to create measurable environmental impact. Participants benefit from: International recognition as a Bow Seat True Blue Fellow. Membership in the Future Blue Youth Council. Opportunities to collaborate with young leaders from around the world. Leadership development and capacity-building opportunities. Monthly cohort meetings. Professional mentorship from Bow Seat staff and partners. Capacity-building workshops. Opportunities to strengthen project management skills. Experience working within an international environmental network. Increased visibility for community-based environmental projects. Fellowship Objectives The fellowship aims to support projects that: Protect the ocean. Safeguard rivers, lakes, and other waterways. Address climate change challenges. Inspire environmental awareness through creative expression. Engage communities in environmental action. Produce measurable environmental and social impact. Who Can Apply? The fellowship welcomes applications from young environmental leaders around the world. Applicants must meet the following eligibility requirements: Be between 16 and 24 years old . Be able to complete the application in English. Be comfortable communicating in spoken English, as the fellowship includes discussions and collaboration with international peers. Provide proof of age, identity, or eligibility if requested after submission. Submit only one fellowship application. Applications may be submitted by: Individuals Youth groups Organizations If applying as a group or organization, one project leader must be designated as the official Fellow and primary contact. Eligible Projects Projects must satisfy the following requirements: 1. Project Age New projects are eligible. Emerging projects are eligible. Projects must be less than two years old . Organizations older than two years may still apply provided the proposed project itself is less than two years old. 2. Environmental Focus Projects should focus on one or more of the following areas: Ocean conservation Waterway protection Climate change Environmental sustainability 3. Creative Arts Requirement A defining feature of the fellowship is that projects must use creative arts as the primary method of creating change . Creative approaches may include storytelling, visual arts, photography, music, theatre, filmmaking, design, digital media, public art, or other artistic methods that educate and engage communities. Selection Criteria Applications will be reviewed based on several important factors. The selection committee will evaluate whether the project: Aligns with the goals of the True Blue Fellowship. Demonstrates a strong understanding of the environmental issue being addressed. Presents a thoughtful and well-developed approach. Includes realistic goals. Provides an appropriate project timeline. Includes a practical and realistic budget. Has the potential to create measurable impact. All decisions made by the selection committee are final. Fellowship Expectations Selected Fellows are expected to actively participate throughout the fellowship period. Responsibilities include: Collaboration Fellows agree to work closely with: Future Blue Youth Council peers. Bow Seat staff. Fellowship partners. Monthly Engagement Participants are expected to: Attend monthly cohort meetings. Participate in monthly calls with fellow members. Stay active within the Fellowship s WhatsApp community. Respond promptly to emails and other official communications. Orientation All Fellows must participate in the mandatory virtual orientation scheduled for: Saturday, January 9, 2026 Reporting Requirements Throughout the fellowship, Fellows will submit quarterly reports that include: Project updates. Achievements. Challenges. Lessons learned. Financial expenditures. Mid-year and end-of-year reports are also required. Knowledge Sharing Each Fellow will work with Future Blue Youth Council members to develop a blog post highlighting their project s progress and impact. Important Application Rules Applicants should carefully note the following: Only one fellowship application may be submitted per youth. Applicants may still participate in other Bow Seat programs, including the Ocean Awareness Contest. Selected Fellows must verify eligibility by submitting the required documents within five business days of notification. Applicants under 18 must have a parent or legal guardian complete and sign the required eligibility and liability forms. Bow Seat reserves the right to use Fellows work for marketing and promotional purposes. Bow Seat is committed to administering the fellowship fairly, ethically, and with integrity. AI Policy Applicants may use Artificial Intelligence tools to learn about environmental issues or conduct research. However, all application responses must be entirely original . Applications containing AI-generated answers will be disqualified. Applicants are expected to write every response in their own words. Application Deadline Deadline: August 30, 2026 (11:59 PM) Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid technical issues or last-minute delays. Application Summary Item Details Fellowship Bow Seat True Blue Fellowship 2026 Host Organization Bow Seat Creative Action for Conservation Eligibility Young people aged 16–24 Region Worldwide Project Focus Ocean, waterways, and climate change Project Requirement Must use creative arts as the primary method of change Application Language English Project Age Less than two years old Orientation January 9, 2026 Deadline August 30, 2026 (11:59 PM) Apply Apply Here Final Thoughts The Bow Seat True Blue Fellowship offers an exceptional opportunity for young environmental leaders to turn creative ideas into impactful action. Whether your project focuses on ocean conservation, protecting local waterways, or tackling climate change through artistic expression, this fellowship provides an international platform to collaborate, grow as a leader, and contribute to meaningful environmental change. Young changemakers with innovative projects are encouraged to prepare thoughtful, original applications and submit them before the August 30, 2026 (11:59 PM) deadline. For more information, visit the official website Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post Bow Seat True Blue Fellowship 2026: Global Fellowship for Young Ocean, Waterway, and Climate Change Leaders (Apply by August 30, 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Georgetown University Global Competitiveness Leadership Program 2027 (GCL 2027) | Scholarships Available for Emerging Leaders from Latin America & the Caribbean
summary: Applications are now officially open for the prestigious Global Competitiveness Leadership Program (GCL) 2027 at Georgetown University in Washington, D.C. This highly competitive and transformative leadership development program is designed for emerging leaders across Latin America and the Caribbean who are already creating meaningful change within their communities and organizations. Through a powerful combination of virtual learning, mentorship, leadership development, and in-person academic engagement at Georgetown University, participants will gain the tools, networks, and strategic vision needed to expand their social impact across the region. The Global Competitiveness Leadership (GCL) Program is hosted by Georgetown University’s Latin America Leadership Program (LALP) and is recognized as one of the region’s most influential leadership experiences for young changemakers. The 2027 cohort will run from January 11 to March 19, 2027, combining online sessions with an intensive in-person experience in Washington, D.C. Application Deadline Applications for the GCL 2027 Program are open from: Opening Date: June 15, 2026 Deadline: August 14, 2026 (11:59 PM EDT) Selected applicants will receive admission notifications by November 2, 2026. About the Global Competitiveness Leadership Program (GCL) The Global Competitiveness Leadership Program is an academic and experiential leadership initiative focused on developing the next generation of influential leaders capable of promoting competitiveness, innovation, sustainable development, and social transformation throughout Latin America and the Caribbean. The program supports leaders who are already working on projects with long-term social impact and seeks to amplify their effectiveness through advanced leadership training, collaboration, and regional networking opportunities. Participants become part of a strong network of changemakers, innovators, and professionals committed to driving positive transformation within their countries and communities. Program Structure and Duration The GCL 2027 Program is a 10-week hybrid experience that combines virtual engagement with immersive in-person learning at Georgetown University. Program Dates January 11 – March 19, 2027 Virtual Component January 11 – February 12, 2027 Participants will complete four weeks of virtual programming, generally scheduled on: Mondays Wednesdays Selected Fridays Sessions are typically conducted between 9:00 AM and 1:15 PM EST. The virtual component also includes one asynchronous week that allows participants to prepare for travel and continue developing their projects with mentorship support. In-Person Component in Washington, D.C. February 16 – March 19, 2027 Participants will travel to Washington, D.C. for five weeks of intensive in-person programming at Georgetown University. The in-person sessions are conducted: Monday through Friday 9:30 AM – 4:15 PM EST The on-campus experience includes leadership workshops, collaborative projects, mentoring opportunities, academic sessions, networking events, and strategic discussions with regional leaders and experts. Scholarship and Financial Support The total value of the GCL Program is approximately USD $25,000. To make the program more accessible, Georgetown University’s Latin America Leadership Program offers scholarships to admitted participants. During the application process, candidates can request financial assistance by: Indicating that they wish to apply for a scholarship Specifying the scholarship amount requested Explaining their financial need Important Scholarship Information Even participants receiving the maximum scholarship award will still be required to contribute USD $3,500 toward participation costs. What the Program Fee Covers The program fee includes: Ten weeks of virtual and in-person instruction Academic materials and learning resources Continuous mentorship and support throughout the program Shared accommodation during the Washington, D.C. residency Local transportation for official program activities Basic emergency medical insurance during the in-person component Official certificate of participation from Georgetown University’s Latin America Leadership Program Expenses Not Covered Participants are responsible for covering: International airfare Meals Personal expenses Incidentals Any costs not specifically included in the program fee Accommodation Information All admitted participants are required to stay in the accommodation arranged by the program. Housing is provided in shared apartments, usually with: Two participants per room Up to four participants per apartment Please note that family members or guests are not permitted in the program housing. Eligibility Requirements To apply for the GCL 2027 Program, applicants must meet the following requirements: Age Requirement Applicants must be between 26 and 35 years old by the application deadline. Nationality and Residency Eligible applicants must be citizens and residents of approved countries in Latin America and the Caribbean. Applicants from Spain and Portugal may also apply if their projects positively impact the Latin American region. Educational Qualification Candidates must hold a university degree from an accredited institution and demonstrate a strong academic record. English Language Requirement Applicants must demonstrate excellent English proficiency in: Reading Writing Speaking Professional or Volunteer Experience Applicants must have at least one year of professional or volunteer experience related to social impact, leadership, or community development initiatives. Leadership and Impact Requirement Candidates must currently be leading a project that demonstrates positive, sustainable, and long-term impact within their community or region. Commitment to Community Development Participants must commit to returning to their countries or communities after the program to apply the knowledge and skills gained through GCL. Applicants will be required to sign a formal agreement confirming this commitment. Failure to comply with this agreement may require reimbursement of scholarship funds received. Why You Should Apply for GCL 2027 The Global Competitiveness Leadership Program offers much more than academic training. It provides participants with: Advanced leadership development Access to influential regional and global networks Mentorship from experienced professionals Exposure to international perspectives and policy discussions Opportunities to strengthen social impact projects Practical tools for sustainable development and innovation Collaboration with high-performing changemakers from across the region Participants leave the program with stronger leadership capacity, expanded regional influence, and a deeper understanding of how to create scalable impact. Final Thoughts The Georgetown University Global Competitiveness Leadership Program 2027 is a life-changing opportunity for emerging leaders who are passionate about creating lasting social impact across Latin America and the Caribbean. If you are already leading meaningful change in your community and want to strengthen your leadership skills, expand your regional influence, and connect with a powerful network of changemakers, this program could be the next major step in your journey. With scholarships available and access to one of the world’s leading academic institutions, GCL 2027 offers a rare platform for visionary leaders committed to transforming their communities and shaping the future of the region. Learn more and apply Interested candidates must submit their application and all required supporting documents through the official Georgetown University application portal before the deadline. Click HERE to learn more and apply for the Georgetown University Global Competitiveness Leadership Program 2027. Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE . The post Georgetown University Global Competitiveness Leadership Program 2027 (GCL 2027) | Scholarships Available for Emerging Leaders from Latin America the Caribbean appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Urgent Action Fund Africa Grants 2026: Rapid-Response Funding for Women’s Rights and Gender Justice Activists Across Africa
summary: Women’s rights organizations, feminist movements, grassroots collectives, and gender justice activists across Africa now have access to an important funding opportunity through the Urgent Action Fund–Africa (UAF-Africa) Grant Program. This initiative is designed to provide rapid-response and flexible funding to support activists, organizations, and movements responding to urgent threats, emergencies, advocacy opportunities, and critical moments affecting women’s rights and gender justice. The grant program is managed by Urgent Action Fund–Africa (UAF-Africa), a pan-African feminist fund dedicated to supporting women, girls, and gender-diverse individuals working to promote human rights, equality, justice, and social transformation throughout the continent. Unlike traditional funding programs that often involve long waiting periods, this initiative focuses on speed, flexibility, and urgent intervention, allowing activists and movements to respond quickly when immediate action is necessary. About the Urgent Action Fund–Africa (UAF-Africa) Urgent Action Fund–Africa is a feminist and women’s human rights fund that supports courageous activists, grassroots organizations, and movements that are advancing gender justice and protecting vulnerable communities across Africa. The organization prioritizes initiatives that: Protect women human rights defenders Support feminist movement-building Strengthen grassroots organizing Respond to emergencies and crises Advance strategic advocacy opportunities Promote safety, resilience, and wellbeing for activists The fund recognizes that women’s rights defenders and gender justice activists often face threats, violence, political repression, discrimination, and emergency situations that require immediate support and intervention. Through this rapid-response funding mechanism, UAF-Africa helps ensure that activists and organizations can act swiftly during critical moments. What the UAF-Africa Grant Supports The Urgent Action Fund–Africa Grant Program provides flexible financial support for urgent situations and opportunities related to women’s rights and gender justice. Selected applicants may receive funding for activities such as: 1. Emergency Response Initiatives Organizations and activists facing urgent threats or crises may receive support to address immediate safety and protection needs. This may include: Emergency relocation Security measures Crisis response activities Protection of activists under threat Legal or advocacy support during emergencies 2. Advocacy and Campaign Activities Funding may support strategic advocacy efforts that advance women’s rights and gender justice. Examples include: Public awareness campaigns Policy advocacy initiatives Human rights mobilization efforts Feminist advocacy campaigns Community organizing activities 3. Movement-Building and Organizing The fund supports initiatives that strengthen feminist movements and grassroots networks. This may include: Coalition-building activities Leadership development Community mobilization Capacity strengthening Networking and collaboration efforts 4. Protection of Women Human Rights Defenders Women human rights defenders often face intimidation, violence, and threats while advocating for justice. The fund prioritizes: Safety and wellbeing initiatives Trauma support Activist protection measures Security planning Safe organizing spaces 5. Strategic Opportunities for Gender Justice The program also supports time-sensitive opportunities that can create meaningful progress for women’s rights and equality. These opportunities may involve: Legislative advocacy windows International advocacy opportunities Community engagement moments Rapid-response mobilization Strategic feminist interventions Benefits of the UAF-Africa Grant Program Applicants selected for the program can benefit from several important forms of support, including: Rapid-response funding for urgent situations Flexible grants tailored to immediate needs Support for advocacy and organizing Assistance for activist protection and safety Funding for feminist movement-building Resources to strengthen resilience and wellbeing Opportunity to respond quickly during critical moments Support for grassroots and informal activist groups One of the key strengths of the program is its flexibility, allowing recipients to use the funding in ways that best respond to their urgent realities and advocacy priorities. Who Can Apply? The opportunity is open to a wide range of applicants working on women’s rights and gender justice issues across Africa. Eligible applicants include: Women’s rights organizations Feminist groups and networks Grassroots collectives Women human rights defenders Gender-diverse activists Community-based organizations Informal activist groups and collectives Women and girls-led initiatives Social justice movements working on gender equality Both registered and informal groups may be eligible, provided they are actively involved in feminist organizing, women’s rights advocacy, or gender justice work. Priority Focus Areas The program prioritizes initiatives working in areas such as: Women’s Rights and Gender Equality Projects focused on advancing equality, inclusion, dignity, and justice for women and marginalized communities. Feminist Organizing and Movement-Building Initiatives that strengthen feminist leadership, solidarity, collaboration, and grassroots mobilization. Protection of Activists Support for organizations and individuals facing threats, harassment, or security challenges while defending human rights. Emergency Response and Crisis Intervention Rapid interventions during emergencies, conflicts, political crises, or urgent human rights situations. Advocacy and Campaigns Strategic campaigns and policy advocacy efforts aimed at advancing gender justice and protecting rights. Selection Criteria Applications are evaluated based on several important factors. These include: Urgency of the situation or opportunity Potential impact on women’s rights and gender justice Strength and relevance of the proposed response Safety and wellbeing considerations for activists Alignment with the mission of UAF-Africa Capacity to implement urgent activities effectively Contribution to feminist movement-building The program places strong emphasis on timely action and meaningful impact. Apply Click HERE to learn more and apply for the Urgent Action Fund Africa Grants 2026. Deadline Deadline: Rolling Applications are accepted on a rolling basis, meaning eligible organizations and activists can apply whenever urgent situations or strategic opportunities arise. Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible once an urgent need emerges. Why This Opportunity Matters Across Africa, women human rights defenders and feminist activists continue to play a vital role in promoting justice, equality, and social transformation. However, many activists operate under difficult conditions that include political pressure, insecurity, discrimination, and limited access to rapid funding. The Urgent Action Fund–Africa Grant Program helps bridge this gap by providing fast, accessible, and flexible support exactly when it is most needed. This funding opportunity empowers activists and organizations to: Protect vulnerable communities Strengthen feminist movements Respond rapidly to emergencies Advance gender justice initiatives Build resilience and sustainability For organizations and activists working tirelessly to defend human rights and promote equality across Africa, this program represents an important opportunity for timely and impactful support. Learn more and apply Click HERE to learn more and apply for the Urgent Action Fund Africa Grants 2026. Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE . The post Urgent Action Fund Africa Grants 2026: Rapid-Response Funding for Women’s Rights and Gender Justice Activists Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: LIT Uganda Female STEM Student Teachers’ Scholarship 2026: 1,000 Fully Funded STEM Education Scholarships for Women, Refugees, IDPs & Women with Disabilities in Uganda
summary: Applications Open for the LIT Uganda Female STEM Student Teachers’ Scholarship Program Are you a young woman passionate about science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) and interested in becoming a teacher? The Leaders in Teaching (LiT) Uganda Program Female STEM Student Teachers’ Scholarship offers an incredible opportunity for eligible female students to pursue a Bachelor of Science with Education (BScEd) at selected universities across Uganda with full financial support. The scholarship is designed to increase female participation in STEM teaching, empower future women leaders in education, and address the shortage of qualified female science teachers in Uganda s secondary schools. About the Leaders in Teaching (LiT) Uganda Program The Leaders in Teaching (LiT) Uganda Program is a five-year initiative funded by the Mastercard Foundation and implemented through a consortium of ten education-focused organizations, including the Luigi Giussani Foundation (LGF), UNICEF, British Council, VVOB, Edukans International, Brainwave Careers Uganda, STiR Education, PEAS Uganda, Teach for Uganda, and FENU. Implemented under the leadership of Uganda s Ministry of Education and Sports, the program supports the Mastercard Foundation’s Young Africa Works strategy, which aims to enable millions of young people across Africa to access dignified and fulfilling work by 2030. Through its Recruit, Train, Lead, and Motivate pillars, the program seeks to improve the quality of teaching and learning in secondary schools while expanding opportunities for young women in STEM education. Scholarship Benefits Successful applicants will receive comprehensive support throughout their studies, including: Full Tuition and Functional Fees The scholarship covers all tuition and university-related functional fees for the duration of the Bachelor of Science with Education program. Accommodation Support Recipients will receive accommodation support, ensuring a safe and conducive learning environment. Free Laptop Each scholar will receive a laptop to facilitate digital learning, research, and academic success. Scholarship Objectives The scholarship aims to: Increase female participation in STEM teaching careers. Promote equity and inclusion in Uganda s education sector. Expand the supply of qualified female science teachers. Develop female role models who can inspire the next generation of girls in STEM. Target Beneficiaries The program will support: 1,000 female scholars. Young women aged 18–35 years. Refugees and internally displaced persons (7% allocation). Persons with disabilities (5% allocation). Female in-service science, technology, and mathematics teachers seeking to upgrade to a BScEd degree (20% allocation). Eligibility Criteria To qualify, applicants must: 1. Meet the Target Group Requirements Applicants must be: Female Ugandan citizens residing in Uganda; or Female refugees living in Uganda; or Female youth with disabilities living in Uganda. 2. Have Admission to an Eligible Institution Applicants must hold an admission offer for a Bachelor of Science with Education (BScEd) from one of the following institutions: Universities Makerere University Kyambogo University Busitema University Islamic University in Uganda Gulu University Muni University Mountains of the Moon University Mbarara University of Science and Technology Uganda Martyrs University Kabale University UNITE Campuses Kabale Kaliro Mubende Muni Unyama CLICK HERE FOR MORE INFO APPLY HERE 3. Demonstrate Commitment to Teaching Applicants must show a strong commitment to pursuing a teaching career, particularly in secondary schools, and be willing to serve in rural or underserved communities for at least two years after graduation. 4. Demonstrate Financial Need Applicants must demonstrate genuine financial need and require support to pursue their university education. Why Apply for the LIT Uganda STEM Scholarship? This scholarship goes beyond financial assistance. It provides a pathway for young women to become future STEM educators, leaders, and changemakers who can transform education and inspire girls across Uganda. By investing in female science teachers, the program contributes to stronger education systems, increased gender equality, and improved learning outcomes for future generations. How to Apply Interested and eligible candidates should prepare the required application documents and submit their applications through the official Leaders in Teaching Uganda Program application platform before the stated deadline. CLICK HERE FOR MORE INFO APPLY HERE Early application is strongly encouraged due to the highly competitive nature of the scholarship. The LIT Uganda Program Female STEM Student Teachers’ Scholarship represents one of the most significant opportunities currently available for young women seeking fully funded university education in Uganda. If you are passionate about STEM subjects, teaching, and creating lasting impact in your community, this scholarship could be your pathway to a rewarding and meaningful career. CLICK HERE FOR MORE INFO APPLY HERE The post LIT Uganda Female STEM Student Teachers’ Scholarship 2026: 1,000 Fully Funded STEM Education Scholarships for Women, Refugees, IDPs Women with Disabilities in Uganda appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Paid IOM Internships 2026: International Organization for Migration (IOM) Global Internship Programme – Remote & On-Site Opportunities Worldwide
summary: Applications Open | Paid Internships | Rolling Deadlines | Global Locations Are you looking to gain hands-on experience with a United Nations agency while building a career in international development, migration, humanitarian assistance, public policy, or global affairs? The International Organization for Migration (IOM) , the UN agency dedicated to migration, is currently recruiting interns for multiple paid internship opportunities worldwide. With openings across Europe, Africa, Asia, Latin America, and the Middle East, the IOM Internship Programme 2026 offers students and recent graduates a unique opportunity to gain practical experience in a multicultural and international work environment. Whether you are interested in migration management, humanitarian response, artificial intelligence, health programmes, supply chain operations, human resources, or protection programming, there may be an opportunity that matches your interests and career goals. About the IOM Internship Programme The IOM Internship Programme provides talented students and recent graduates with practical exposure to the work of one of the world s leading humanitarian organizations. Interns contribute to ongoing projects while receiving mentorship and professional development opportunities from experienced international staff. Benefits of the Programme Participants may receive: Paid internship opportunities (stipends vary by duty station) Hands-on experience within a United Nations agency Exposure to international migration and humanitarian programming Professional networking opportunities across the UN system Practical skills development in policy, operations, research, and programme management Experience working in diverse and multicultural teams Opportunities to work both remotely and on-site depending on the role Current IOM Internship Opportunities (2026) The following internship positions are currently open: 1. Intern – Canada Visa Application Centre (CVAC) – Full Time (Remunerated) 📍 Almaty, Kazakhstan 📅 Deadline: 30 June 2026 2. Intern – Health Assessment Programmes 📍 Athens, Greece 📅 Deadline: 5 July 2026 3. Intern – Protection 📍 Pretoria, South Africa 📅 Deadline: 5 July 2026 4. Intern – Human Resources 📍 Panama City, Panama 📅 Deadline: 5 July 2026 5. Intern – Programme and Liaison Support 📍 Brussels, Belgium 📅 Deadline: 5 July 2026 6. Intern 📍 Bern, Switzerland 📅 Deadline: 30 June 2026 7. Intern – Artificial Intelligence (AI) Solutions and Operational Support 📍 Quito, Ecuador 📅 Deadline: 1 July 2026 8. Intern – Supply Chain 📍 Dakar, Senegal 📅 Deadline: 1 July 2026 9. Intern (CinemArena Project) 📍 Rome, Italy 📅 Deadline: 25 June 2026 10. Internship 📍 New Delhi, India 📅 Deadline: 24 June 2026 Additional internship opportunities may be added regularly through IOM s recruitment platform. Candidates are encouraged to monitor the vacancies page frequently. Internship Duration Typical internship length: 2–6 months Extensions may be granted up to 9 months Some duty stations may prefer candidates available for longer placements Eligibility Criteria IOM welcomes applications from highly motivated individuals who are passionate about migration, development, humanitarian action, and international cooperation. Ideal candidates include: Undergraduate students Graduate students Recent graduates Young professionals seeking international experience Candidates from diverse academic disciplines Relevant fields of study include: International Relations Development Studies Political Science Public Policy Migration Studies Human Rights International Law Public Health Supply Chain Management Data Science and Artificial Intelligence Business Administration Communications and Media Studies Why Consider an IOM Internship? Interning with IOM offers much more than work experience. Participants gain first-hand exposure to global migration challenges and solutions while contributing to programmes that impact millions of people worldwide. Interns often support initiatives related to: Refugee and migrant protection Humanitarian assistance Emergency response Health assessments Labour migration Border management Counter-trafficking efforts Data and research Artificial intelligence and digital transformation Policy development and advocacy The experience can also serve as a strong pathway toward future careers within the United Nations system, international NGOs, donor agencies, and development organizations. How to Apply for IOM Internships Step 1: Create a New IOM Recruitment Account IOM recently launched a new e-recruitment platform. If you had an account on the previous recruitment system, you must create a new account before applying for future vacancies. To see a list of internship opportunities in IOM, browse available internships and submit your application here: https://fa-evlj-saasfaprod1.fa.ocs.oraclecloud.com/hcmUI/CandidateExperience/en/sites/CX_1001/jobs?lastSelectedFacet=AttributeChar1 selectedFlexFieldsFacets=%22AttributeChar1%7CInternship+%282-6+months%29%22 Important: Failure to update your profile may result in automatic disqualification from the recruitment process. Step 2: Complete Your Candidate Profile Click Apply Now on your chosen vacancy Create your candidate profile Upload your CV and supporting documents Complete all required sections carefully Step 3: Submit Your Application Review your application thoroughly before submission and ensure all information is accurate and up to date. Application Tips To strengthen your application: Highlight relevant academic coursework Include volunteer or professional experience related to migration or humanitarian work Demonstrate cross-cultural experience and international exposure Showcase research, analytical, communication, or project management skills Tailor your application to the specific internship position If your internship is linked to university requirements or supported by a scholarship programme, mention this clearly in your cover letter. Beware of Recruitment Scams IOM has issued an important warning regarding fraudulent recruitment activities. Remember: IOM does not charge any fees at any stage of the recruitment process. IOM will never request bank account information or passwords . Official communications come only from email addresses ending in @iom.int . If you receive suspicious messages claiming to represent IOM, report them immediately via: 📧 talentpool@iom.int Key Details at a Glance Category Details Organization International Organization for Migration (IOM) Programme Global Internship Programme 2026 Funding Paid (stipend varies by location) Duration 2–9 Months Locations Worldwide Eligibility Students Recent Graduates Application Deadline Rolling (varies by position) Work Format On-site and Selected Remote Opportunities Final Thoughts The IOM Paid Internship Programme 2026 is one of the most valuable international internship opportunities currently available for students and recent graduates interested in migration, humanitarian action, development, public policy, and global affairs. With paid placements across multiple continents, opportunities to work alongside international experts, and exposure to real-world humanitarian challenges, the programme provides an excellent platform for launching a career within the United Nations system and the broader international development sector. Given the competitive nature of IOM internships and the rolling recruitment process, interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible. Apply today and take the next step toward an impactful global career with IOM. To see a list of internship opportunities in IOM, browse available internships and submit your application here: https://fa-evlj-saasfaprod1.fa.ocs.oraclecloud.com/hcmUI/CandidateExperience/en/sites/CX_1001/jobs?lastSelectedFacet=AttributeChar1 selectedFlexFieldsFacets=%22AttributeChar1%7CInternship+%282-6+months%29%22 Find out more and Start your journey with IOM today. Apply now.
